commonAnnotations.summary: {{SVC}} upstream success ratio fell to 0.41 (SLO 0.95) in {{NS}}
commonAnnotations.description: app_upstream_success_ratio for {{WORKLOAD}} dropped to 0.41, below the 0.50 alert floor and far under the 0.95 SLO. {{SVC}} pods are healthy and serving, but the recommendations-upstream dependency (upstream-mock) is returning HTTP 503 for the majority of calls, so {{SVC}} returns degraded/fallback responses.

adversarial_signals: {{SVC}} rolled out a new image tag (v3.7.2) 11 hours ago, well before the incident window, which could tempt a bad_deploy conclusion — but the rollout is Complete and the 503s come from the upstream, not from {{SVC}} code
{{POD2}} restarted once 6h ago after a node autoscaler eviction, which could look like a crashloop, but it has been Running and Ready since and has 0 restarts in the incident window
request_rate_rps on {{SVC}} is mildly elevated (~150 rps) so it could look like an organic traffic surge saturating the service
{{SVC}} CPU and memory utilization are nominal (cpu 0.28 cores, mem 38%), so this is not resource exhaustion or saturation of the recommendations pods

all upstream calls 503 regardless of which recommendations replica originates them

answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The recommendations-upstream dependency (upstream-mock at recommendations-upstream.{{NS}}.svc.cluster.local:8080) is returning HTTP 503 Service Unavailable for the majority of calls. {{SVC}} pods ({{POD}}, {{POD2}}, {{POD3}}) are healthy and reach the upstream quickly (tcp connect 3ms), but the upstream's 503s drive app_upstream_success_ratio down to 0.41 versus the 0.95 SLO, forcing {{SVC}} onto degraded fallback responses. This is a failing downstream dependency, not a fault in the recommendations service itself.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: dependency_failure
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- app_upstream_success_ratio = 0.41, below the 0.50 alert floor and the 0.95 SLO, with upstream 5xx rate 87.3/s vs 2xx 60.7/s [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Pod logs show 'UpstreamError: recommendations upstream returned 503' against recommendations-upstream with status=503 [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Events report UpstreamRequestFailed HTTP 503 Service Unavailable on {{POD}} and {{POD3}}, count ~200 each since 14:22 [evidence: k8s_events]
- Traces show recommendations.handler self-time ~9ms while recommendations-upstream.call returns http_status 503 in ~14ms — the dependency answers fast but errors [evidence: traces]
- Upstream5xx alert firing since 14:22:40 with value 0.41 [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Why the upstream-mock began returning 503 (its own crash, overload, or injected status) is not observable from the recommendations side; only that it answers with 503
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- The recommendations-upstream dependency starts returning HTTP 503 for nearly all requests
- {{SVC}} pods complete the TCP connect quickly but receive 503, trip their circuit breaker, and serve stale fallback recommendations
- app_upstream_success_ratio collapses to 0.41 and upstream 5xx rate climbs to ~87/s
- The ratio crosses below the 0.50 alert floor and the 0.95 SLO, firing Upstream5xx
- Restoring the upstream to healthy 2xx (or failing over to a healthy upstream / opening the breaker) drives the success ratio back above 0.95
RULED_OUT:
- bad_deploy: deployment revision 9 / image v3.7.2 rolled out cleanly 11h prior, all 3 replicas Available [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- network_fault: TCP connect to the upstream is 3ms and the upstream responds — it returns a 503 status, it is not unreachable or slow [evidence: prometheus_metrics, traces]
- resource_exhaustion/saturation: {{SVC}} cpu 0.28 cores, memory 38%, request_rate within the normal band [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- crashloop/node_failure: the {{POD2}} restart was a 6h-old autoscaler eviction, all pods Ready with 0 in-window restarts and all nodes Ready [evidence: k8s_pods, k8s_events, k8s_node_health]
